H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, sd=1.78, confidence=strong. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed, no bimodality. Qualitative (29) show strong music liking and settle times around 5–10 minutes. YouTube views=30 – the highest among HEALING, though retention data missing. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, close to observed, confirming efficacy.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: A large group feels calmer quickly and really likes the sound, though few have discovered it yet.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, sd=1.73, confidence=strong. Outcome distribution tight; qualitative (21) indicate immediate settle for some listeners. YouTube views=5, low discovery despite solid scores. AutoGluon prediction is similar to observed.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Most users notice relief from overstimulation, though music preference is split.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, sd=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Slight right‑skew, consistent with other OS protocols. Qualitative (23) note noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon aligns with observed mean.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: People generally feel balanced and calmer, with quick settling for most.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: The protocol recorded n=67, mean=7.22, sd=1.93, confidence=strong. The outcome distribution shows a modest right‑skew without clear bimodality. Qualitative responses (23) highlight moderate to significant improvement for most listeners, with settle times clustering at 5–10 minutes and music rated positively. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views), indicating low discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.84, modestly higher than observed, suggesting potential under‑utilisation of adviser routing.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most people feel noticeably better after a few minutes, and they generally like the music.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, sd=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Distribution is fairly tight; no bimodal pattern evident. Qualitative data (20) report noticeable improvement for many, though music opinions are mixed. YouTube views are low (6). AutoGluon predicts a lower score than for L1, aligning with observed modest efficacy.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: The protocol helps many with anxiety, though some find the music just okay.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, sd=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Outcome spread is similar to L1/L2, with a slight right‑skew. Qualitative feedback (20) shows most notice improvement, with a few reporting no effect and longer settle times (≈10 min). YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon’s prediction aligns closely with observed mean.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Most listeners notice improvement, but a few don’t feel any change and it may take longer to settle.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, sd=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Distribution modestly left‑skewed, reflecting lower baseline in crisis contexts. Qualitative responses (24) emphasize significant improvement for many, despite modest scores. YouTube reach is 6 views. AutoGluon flags H‑CR‑L2 as top crisis performer (predicted 7.96), indicating possible routing refinement.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments many report strong relief quickly, even if the rating sounds modest.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, sd=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Outcome distribution similar to L1, with slight improvement. Qualitative (16) note mixed music opinion but effective rhythm. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts high efficacy (7.96), suggesting this protocol may be under‑exposed.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners often feel calmer during acute distress, though music opinions vary.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, sd=1.91, confidence=meaningful. The highest variance among crisis protocols (sd 1.91) hints at a subgroup effect. Qualitative (19) report fast settling for many but also some non‑responders. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon prediction aligns with L2, highlighting a need to explore hold‑phase extension.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Fast relief is reported by many, but responses are mixed, suggesting a subgroup effect.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, sd=1.86, confidence=strong. Distribution modestly right‑skewed. Qualitative (15) report quick settle (5 min) and high music approval. YouTube views=4 suggest limited reach. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, slightly higher than observed, indicating possible under‑exposure.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Many find this helps them wind down fast, and the music is well‑liked.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, sd=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Tightest variance in sleep series. Qualitative (15) note mixed music opinions and some listeners finding rhythm busy. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon prediction close to observed.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Some users benefit, but a few feel the rhythm is a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, sd=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Distribution similar to L1/L2. Qualitative (22) show balanced rhythm appreciation. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts near observed level.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Most listeners settle quickly and find the rhythm comfortable, even if the music isn’t a favorite.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, sd=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Left‑skewed; qualitative (23) note busy rhythm for some listeners. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts higher, suggesting rhythm smoothing.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Some gain focus, but the rhythm can feel slightly busy for several listeners.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, sd=1.60, confidence=meaningful. Moderate right‑skew; qualitative (17) show balanced improvement. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon aligns.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Many experience clearer focus and enjoy the sound, though not everyone loves it.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, sd=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Wide spread but top THRIVING score; qualitative (29) confirm strong improvements. YouTube views=15. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, confirming high efficacy.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Most report strong concentration gains and really like the music.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, sd=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Distribution modest; qualitative (21) show many slight improvements. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed mean.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: A few notice a slight boost in creativity, but many don’t feel a big change.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, sd=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Tight distribution; qualitative (15) show strong music love. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon consistent.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: More people report creative flow, and many love the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, sd=1.32, confidence=developing. Right‑skewed with high positivity. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon matches observed.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: This is the most helpful for creativity, with many noticing clear benefits.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, sd=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Tight distribution; qualitative (23) note long settle times for many. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon aligns.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners often feel balanced, but many need a longer time to settle.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, sd=2.03, confidence=meaningful. High variance; qualitative (16) show mixed outcomes. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon aligns.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Responses vary widely, with some feeling better and others not much changed.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, sd=1.73, confidence=developing. Small sample; qualitative (19) report non‑settlers. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, higher than observed.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Most notice a positive shift, though a few don’t settle at all.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, sd=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Slight left‑skew; qualitative (22) note many improvements but slower settle. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, higher than observed.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Most feel a modest lift in mood, though settling can be slower.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, sd=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Narrow distribution; qualitative (15) show significant improvements. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, again above observed.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners generally notice a slight mood lift and like the music.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, sd=1.74, confidence=developing. Slight left‑skew; qualitative (25) report improvements but lower scores. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, highlighting redesign need.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Improvement is modest and some users feel less effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, sd=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Wide spread; qualitative (17) shows improvement despite low mean. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, suggesting retention or measurement gaps.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Many notice improvement, but overall scores are lower than expected.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, sd=1.63, confidence=developing. Moderate spread; qualitative (21) note long settle times. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, indicating pacing adjustments may help.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Some benefit, though settling can take longer for a few users.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, sd=2.38, confidence=developing. High variance, possible polarization. Qualitative (17) report moderate gains. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, suggesting potential if variance reduced.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners who respond positively report a good boost, but reactions are polarized.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, sd=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Slight right‑skew; qualitative (25) include two worsening reports. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly higher.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Most people feel a modest mental boost, though a couple said it made things worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, sd=1.64, confidence=developing. Slight right‑skew; qualitative (20) emphasize calming rhythm. YouTube views=12. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, indicating potential uplift.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners generally feel a slight improvement and find the rhythm calming.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, sd=2.45, confidence=developing. Distribution appears bimodal with a low‑score tail; qualitative (17) report mixed rhythm and two negative outcomes. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a strong mismatch flagging redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Responses are mixed with some feeling worse; overall impact is low.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across the three series the data show generally positive outcomes with clear patterns that match design intent.
• HEALING delivers the highest mean (6.98) and lowest variance, especially in anxiety and overstimulation protocols.
• THRIVING shows strong peaks in focus (T-FC-L3) and creative flow (T-CF-L3) but with more spread.
• TRANSFORMING records the lowest mean (6.59) and highest variance, reflecting its low‑affect, ascending‑arc purpose.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series exhibits outcome profiles aligned with its therapeutic role.
• HEALING protocols are consistent and broadly effective, with most descent designs scoring 7+ and low dispersion.
• THRIVING protocols reach higher individual peaks (e.g., T-FC-L3 7.63) yet show moderate variance, suggesting benefit for specific cognitive targets.
• TRANSFORMING protocols show the greatest variance and lower averages, consistent with their aim toward subtle mood and motivation shifts.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from expected patterns, flagging possible subgroup effects or design issues.
• X-MC-L3 (mean 5.7, sd 2.45) is the lowest scorer and most variable, suggesting a design‑fit problem.
• H-CR-L3 shows high variance (sd 1.91) and a modest positivity rate, indicating heterogeneous crisis responses.
• T-MB-L2 displays an unusually high sd 2.03, pointing to responder heterogeneity.
These anomalies merit targeted investigation to uncover underlying causes.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Several actionable opportunities emerge from the convergent evidence.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1 – AutoGluon predicts 7.84 (under‑delivered 7.22). Consider elevating adviser routing priority for anxiety entries.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 – Strong outcome (7.63) with modest reach (15 views). Increase visibility and test an extended hold phase.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 – Predicted 8.23 but observed 6.74; cross‑bucket routing suggests trans‑diagnostic potential – pilot mixed‑zone recommendations.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L1 – Predicted 7.88 vs observed 6.36; investigate retention barriers and consider rhythmic pacing adjustments.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-CR-L3 – High variance suggests a subgroup; add a brief hold segment to deepen entrainment.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L3 – Lower mean (6.28) with developing confidence; redesign with richer rhythmic complexity.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-MB-L1 – Long settle times (20 min) for many; add listener‑baseline screening to segment high‑energy users.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal both stability concerns and useful similarity patterns.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 15 protocols show similarity < 0.7, indicating notable analytic shifts over recent runs.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Sleep (H‑SL‑L1/L2) and overstimulation (H‑OS‑L3) cluster together; T‑FC‑L1 ↔ T‑FC‑L2 are most similar (0.878).
• Confidence: Embedding data span 15+ runs, providing a solid basis despite observed drift for many protocols.
